name: Grand Junction
description_short: Grand Junction Federal Credit Union is a Colorado-based credit union established in 1957, serving over 5,400 members with $91.8M in assets and a single branch location.
description_long:
Grand Junction Federal Credit Union was established in 1957 and is headquartered in Grand Junction, Colorado. As a member-owned financial cooperative, it has grown to steward over $91.8 million in assets while maintaining a focused community presence with a single main office branch.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of banking products and services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, and business loans. Members have access to online banking, mobile app banking for iOS and Android, bill pay, e-statements, mobile payments, wire transfers, and financial calculators. The institution also provides IRA/KEOGH accounts, drive-thru teller services, and financial counseling to its membership base of over 5,400 members supported by approximately 16 dedicated employees.

What distinguishes Grand Junction FCU is its deep community roots spanning nearly seven decades and its focus on serving a specific geographic community in Colorado. The credit union emphasizes accessibility through multiple account types, with notable populations in regular savings accounts (6,300+), share draft accounts (3,400+), share certificates/CDs (700+), and money market accounts (300+). The institution offers competitive mortgage options including balloon/hybrid mortgages and specialized auto lending for new and used vehicles.

However, prospective members should note that the institution experienced recent service disruptions, with both lobby and drive-thru hours listed as currently closed and website/app status indicating potential service issues at the time of data collection. The single-branch footprint and 0 ATMs limit physical accessibility compared to larger regional or national institutions. Membership eligibility is restricted to those within the specific geographic service community, and there are currently no member reviews available on the platform to assess satisfaction or service quality. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings Accounts (Regular Savings and Money Market)
pros: Established credit union with 67+ years of history serving the Grand Junction community
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, CDs, credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, and business loans
Mobile banking apps available for both iOS and Android devices
Multiple account types with significant member populations (6,300+ savings, 3,400+ share draft, 700+ CDs)
Offers specialized services like financial counseling, wire transfers, and financial calculators
Member-owned cooperative structure with over 5,400 active members
Mortgage options including balloon/hybrid mortgages and competitive auto lending
cons: Single branch location with 0 ATMs limits physical accessibility and convenience
Recent service disruptions reported with lobby and drive-thru listed as currently closed
Membership eligibility restricted to specific geographic community in Grand Junction area
No member reviews available on the platform to evaluate service quality or member satisfaction
Website and app experiencing reported service issues at time of data collection
best_for: Grand Junction, Colorado residents seeking community-focused credit union banking
Small business owners in the local area looking for business loans and commercial services
Individuals seeking personalized financial counseling and relationship banking
diagnosis: Grand Junction FCU is best suited for Colorado residents in the Grand Junction area seeking traditional full-service banking from an established community credit union with competitive lending products. Primary caveat: recent service disruptions and lack of ATM access may frustrate members seeking 24/7 accessibility, and the single-branch footprint makes it impractical for those needing multiple physical locations.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
